texto: Why do you like show jumping?""... Beauty and excitement. The elements of trust, talent, training, love, and danger make show jumping a thrilling and aesthetic experience. It's really the ultimate test of two nervous systems--the kinetic transfer of the rider's muscle to the horse's muscle enables them to clear those jumps. And there's nothing like it--horse and rider forming an arc of beauty, efficiency, and power, like a double helix.""DNA,""Yes, DNA, the code to life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ainslie Sheridan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Why do you like show jumping?""... Beauty and excitement. The elements of trust, talent, training…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, animal, equestrian pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e equestrian.
